codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, the transcript skeleton for worker session `79a8fa20-6de4-4122-850e-4b68fa8410b0`, and commit `3ca12aeda1a3184b5c6b8e7a02fbeb767069e2a0`. The implementation keeps the product change narrowly scoped to the snippets library surface by replacing the nested snippet list with a single `LazyColumn`, which matches the user-facing navigation path the real-fixture tests exercise. The updated instrumentation now directly proves the required history behaviors on the shipped app shell against the live SSH fixture: failed saved-host dispatches do not create success history, successful runs appear newest-first with target labels, and relaunch/deletion coherence still holds. I did not find any high-confidence correctness issues in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SSH terminal worker skill's step 7 still frames lifecycle verification as a manual exercise, but this feature's accepted proof path used connected instrumentation against the real app shell and live SSH fixture because exec-mode workers cannot perform separate human-driven interaction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/root/workspace/github/jtsang4/aterm/.factory/skills/ssh-terminal-worker/SKILL.md` step 7 requires manual lifecycle exercise; the handoff `/root/.factory/missions/91fdc83e-53cf-4a4e-94a9-0be6de23fe64/handoffs/2026-04-14T03-28-12-622Z__snippet-real-fixture-proof-assertions__79a8fa20-6de4-4122-850e-4b68fa8410b0.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` with the explicit suggestion to clarify that exec-mode workers may satisfy this with connected instrumentation evidence when it drives the live app shell and fixture.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the mission guidance, architecture, SSH-terminal worker skill, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit `3ca12aeda1a3184b5c6b8e7a02fbeb767069e2a0`, and I found no high-confidence correctness defects in this feature. The only follow-up I noted is a shared-state skill gap: the current skill text should better describe exec-mode instrumentation as an acceptable substitute for manual lifecycle proof when it still drives the real app shell and live SSH fixture.
